data.title: Rocket agrees to buy Redfin for $1.75B
data.description:
Rocket Cos. (NYSE:RKT), parent of Rocket Mortgage, agreed to acquire real estate broker Redfin (NASDAQ:RDFN) in a deal valued at $1.75B that will combine one of the most-visited brokerage websites with the largest mortgage lender, the companies said Monday.

The online lending platform will pay $12.50 per RDFN share in an all stock transaction, representing a premium of ~63% over the volume-weighted average of the stock for 30 days through Friday. Under the terms, each share of Redfin (NASDAQ:RDFN) common stock will be exchanged for a fixed ratio of 0.7926 shares of Rocket Cos. (NYSE:RKT) class A common stock.

Redfin (RDFN) stock surged 81% to $10.52 in premarket trading, while Rocket (RKT) shares slid 8.4%.

Redfin's (RDFN) home search platform lists more than 1M for-sale and rental listings, as well as a tech-powered brokerage network of more than 2,200 agents. The purchase would add to Rocket's (RKT) pipeline of potential borrowers for its mortgages business.

"Together, we will improve the experience by connecting traditionally disparate steps of the search and financing process with leading technology that removes friction, reduces costs and increases value to American homebuyers," said Rocket CEO Varun Krishna.

The transaction is expected to add to Rocket's (RKT) adjusted EPS by the end of 2026 and to produce more than $200M in run-rate synergies by 2027.

As part of the deal, Rocket (RKT) will collapse its current Up-C structure to simplify its capital structure. Following the collapse, the public stockholders will continue to hold their current shares of common stock, while Dan Gilbert and the other stockholders of Rock Holdings Inc. ("RHI") will hold shares of common stock directly in Rocket Companies, instead of through RHI, and will no longer hold limited liability company units of Rocket, LLC.

In connection with the Up-C collapse, the Rocket (RKT) board declared a cash dividend of $0.80 per share to holders of Rocket's class A common stock to be paid on April 3, 2025, to holders of record at the close of March 20, 2025.

The deal is expected to close in Q2 or Q3 of 2025.

Conference call at 8:00 AM ET.

data.title: Shell declares EUR 0.3315 dividend
data.description:
• Shell (NYSE:SHEL) declared EUR 0.3315/share quarterly dividend.
  • Shareholders have been able to elect to receive their dividends in US dollars, euros or pounds sterling. Holders of ordinary shares who have validly submitted US dollars, euros or pounds sterling currency elections by February 28, 2025 will be entitled to a dividend of US$0.358, €0.3315 or 27.79p per ordinary share, respectively.
  • Payable March 24; for shareholders of record Feb. 14; ex-div Feb. 14.
  • See SHEL Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Rocket Companies declares $0.80 dividend
data.description:
• Rocket Companies (NYSE:RKT) declared $0.80/share special dividend.
  • Payable April 3; for shareholders of record March 20; ex-div March 20.
  • See RKT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Brad Schwartz joins First Bank as CFO
data.description:
First National (NASDAQ:FXNC) Monday said that Brad E. Schwartz will join First National Corporation and First Bank as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer effective March 31, 2025.

Brad brings over 40 years of experience in banking in Virginia and is retired President and COO of TowneBank and formerly CEO, CFO, and COO of Monarch Bank before its acquisition by TowneBank.

At First National, he will join Dennis Dysart, EVP and COO, and Scott Harvard, President and CEO, as named executive officers.

data.title: Alkami intends to offer $300M convertible senior notes offering
data.description:
• Alkami Technology (NASDAQ:ALKT) said it intends to offer $300M aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering.
  • The firm also expects to grant the initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase, for settlement, up to an additional $45M aggregate principal amount of the notes.
  • The notes will be senior, unsecured obligations of Alkami, will accrue interest payable semi-annually in arrears, and will mature on March 15, 2030.
  • Shares -2.1% in premarket trade.

data.title: Smartkem names Jonathan Watkins as COO
data.description:
• Smartkem (NASDAQ:SMTK) said it has appointed Jonathan Watkins as chief operating officer. 
  • Watkins has over three decades of experience, and from July 2024 until his appointment as COO, he served as a consultant to the company.

data.title: NorthWest Healthcare Properties reports Q4 results
data.description:
• NorthWest Healthcare Properties press release (OTC:NWHUF): Q4 FFO of C$0.10.
  • Revenue from investment properties was C$102.7 million for Q4 2024, a decrease of 17.2% from Q4 2023 driven by the disposition of non-core assets during both 2023 and 2024, partially offset by strong same property revenue growth.

data.title: Yum China names Adrian Ding as CFO
data.description:
• Yum China (NYSE:YUMC) said it has appointed Adrian Ding as its finance chief, effective on March 6.
  • Ding has been serving as acting CFO since October 2024.
  • "Over the last five months, he (Ding) has made significant contributions, proving himself as the ideal candidate to serve as the Chief Financial Officer of Yum China. He has effectively led the finance team, strengthened our engagement with stakeholders, including the capital markets, and played a pivotal role in building Yum China's growth momentum," said Joey Wat, CEO of Yum China.
  • Ding had joined the firm in March 2019 as the vice president of Corporate Finance and served as Chief Investment Officer from February 2020 to March 2025.

data.title: DoorDash rallies after S&P 500 Index inclusion
data.description:
DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) moved higher in early trading on Monday after S&P Dow Jones Indices announced the stock will be added to the S&P 500 Index on March 24. Due to the over $10 trillion indexed or benchmarked to the S&P 500 globally as the world’s most widely followed index, the index inclusion is considered a positive catalyst for DASH shares. 

DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) became eligible for S&P inclusion by recording its second quarter of GAAP profitability, and exceeding break-even for the four trailing quarters of GAAP profit.

Bank of America also pointed to positive recent data for DoorDash (DASH) in its recent Gig Data Dive. The firm considers Buy-rated DoorDash (DASH) one of the best-TAM expansion stories in the Internet sector. "We also remain constructive given: 1) NV-driven upside to top-line could be durable for several quarters, with a path to positive NV unit economics; 2) Ad benefit just getting started, earlier stage vs peers; 3) Incremental margin expectations not elevated vs peers," noted analyst Michael McGovern.

Shares of DoorDash (DASH) rallied 4.8% in premarket trading on Monday to $186.74 vs. the 52-week range of $99.32 to $215.25. Short interest on the online food delivery stock stands at 2.4% of the total float.

data.title: Ford plans to inject up to $4.8B into its struggling German subsidiary
data.description:
Ford Motor (NYSE:F) announced on Monday that the company will inject up to 4.4 billion euros ($4.76 billion) into its Ford-Werke German arm as it looks to spark the European business.

"By recapitalising our German operations, we are supporting the transformation of our business in Europe and strengthening our ability to compete with a fresh product portfolio," stated Ford (NYSE:F) Vice Chairman John Lawler. "To build a sustainable business in Europe, we also need to continue to simplify our governance, reduce costs and drive efficiencies," he added.

Ford-Werke GmbH currently operates major plants in Cologne and Saarlouis. The subsidiary is involved in the production of vehicles like the Fiesta and Focus, as well as engine manufacturing.

Shares of Ford Motor (F) were down 1.4% in premarket trading on Monday. The auto stock is down about 20% over the last 52 weeks. The dividend yield for new buyers of the stock is 7.58%.
